## [00:00 - 03:00]

So, this video is going to go over disqualifying people at different red flags that we obviously look out for when it comes to disqualifying people that wouldn't be a good fit for our clients or for our pipeline and then also going over what to actually say to these people and how to wrap up the phone call. So, essentially, obviously, one of the main reasons for this is we just don't want people that wouldn't be a good fit in our pipeline. There are people that come through, they might have an all right application or good application, might be worth an interview, but when you hop on the interview with them, it might turn out that they actually won't be a good fit for it could be any one of these reasons or other ones. So, one thing that we're super, super strict on is them not watching the three minute video. So, the reason why that is is because they quite literally get two emails with the link to the video, county invitation reminders to watch this video and on top of that, they're also actually answering a question saying that they're going to watch it and you can quite literally see here, for example, when they come through and actually book in this call, they're asked this question, will you watch the three minute video before your interview? It is required. Copy and paste this link now and they say yes, but at the end, they still and they are they hop on the interview and they still don't watch it. So, we DQ these people unless they have like a really, really, really, really, really good reason as to why that's the case and why they didn't watch it, but nine times out of ten, they're going to be a DQ because if they can't follow these simple, simple instructions, then that's not going to be good for them and it's not going to be good for us and it's not going to look good on us either when we present these kind of people to our clients. Once again, obviously, you are your discernment and your discernment is what determines who gets into the pipeline and who gets introduced to our clients. So, remember that you're the kind of the gatekeeper to the kingdom, if you will. So, from there, obviously with people like this, obviously you're going to DQ them, but with where you're at now, obviously just getting ramped up with us, go through the interview process just as normal, just to get the reps in until we tell you not to and just dig into them super, super deeply as to why they didn't watch the video and then from there, obviously, go ahead and DQ them. Here are some different red flags that we look out for. Obviously, there's that three minute video there that we just talked about. Then from there, we go into why they didn't know show. There might be some people where they know show and then they reschedule. You want to be super, super skeptical, if not even more skeptical than you normal would be of people like that, why they rescheduled. People that aren't dressed appropriately or they have things in the background all over the place. There was someone, I think, Savanna asked. Savanna was telling me that she interviewed someone who literally had underwear hanging out of their drawer. People like that, you want to notice things like that. People that aren't prepared just come on.

## [03:00 - 06:02]

They're all over the place. People that are bad lighting, they have their windows in the corner of their camera and you quite literally can't see their face because it's all just bad light or it's way too bright or you just can't see the model like they have absolutely no light. We want to call people out on things like that. Background noise. There's either babies in the back screaming or whatever the case may be. People in the back moving around in the middle of something or whatever the case may be. You want to watch out for that. Obviously, full-time availability, which we talked about, it's a red flag. People having their own business, you want to dive into that. Obviously, why they could just continue to grow their own business, but why are they looking for a sales role instead of growing and continuing to grow their own business? Other than that, their energy and their ambition. If people come on and they're super monotone and their energy sucks and they're talking to you like this, you can just see that they almost don't really want to be on the interview. When they go and interview with their clients, it's just going to be a waste of everyone's time because they're not going to be a good fit. That's that their energy. If they have super, super low energy, I just disqualify them because they don't even seem like they're excited and they want to be on this interview. If they then go and interview with one of our clients, what is that going to show? Keep that in mind as well as their ambition. If you sense that they're not super hungry at all, they won't do the work or they're lazy or whatever the case may be, that's obviously another reason there as well. Obviously, them, if they show up in their car or if they're driving, I've had people do that. Obviously, they show up in their car and they have a good reason to do that, then that makes sense. You just want to mention and let them know that, hey, when you interview with one of our clients, just make sure that you are on your computer, whatever the case may be, and that you're not in the car.
